intro: Pocockia orbicularis, also known as the Pocockia plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been reported to provide health benefits in treating various conditions, including wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Pocockia orbicularis include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Pocockia orbicularis is often prepared as a decoction or a topical cream, and is commonly used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as to aid in wound healing.
